frameworks.name: Bayesian Brain
frameworks.one_line: cognition as inference over internal generative models of the world

frameworks.name: modular neural state-space models
frameworks.one_line: learned, recombinable world-model components with amortized neural updates

frameworks.name: amortized inference
frameworks.one_line: neural networks replace iterative inference with learned updates

frameworks.name: compositional generalization
frameworks.one_line: stacking reusable components enables zero-shot transfer across tasks

core_question: Can learned, reusable world-model components implement the Bayesian Brain and support flexible cognition across tasks?
key_assumptions: cognition explains observations through hidden causes
generative structure can be learned rather than pre-specified
inference can be amortized via neural updates
manipulations: task pairs sharing similar latent dynamics
recombination and stacking of trained components
key_findings: modular components recombine across superficially different tasks
framework supports zero-shot generalization to new tasks
predicts selective correlations of inference parameters between tasks
interpretations: learned reusable world-model components are a candidate principle for flexible cognition
scalable realization of Bayesian Brain without task-specific hand-crafting
